subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following combination of features would you observe in grass?
answer_explanation: Grass blades have veins running parallel to each other (parallel venation). Plants with parallel venation generally have a fibrous root system.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following combination of features would you observe in grass?

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following is the correct match between the characteristics of stem and the category of plant?
options: weak stem which cannot stand upright : Creeper
thick, hard stem with branching near the base : Tree
thick, hard stem with branches high on the plant : Herb
answer_key: weak stem which cannot stand upright : Creeper
answer_explanation: Creepers have weak stems that spread on the ground. A green, tender stem belongs to an herb. A thick, hard stem with branching near the base is a shrub. A thick, hard stem with branches high up is a tree.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following is the correct match between the characteristics of stem and the category of plant?

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following is not the primary function of stem?
answer_explanation: Photosynthesis is the primary function of the leaf. However, some stems (like those in cactus) can perform photosynthesis.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following is not the primary function of stem?

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following is not a correct match?
options: Lamina : green flat part of leaf
Margin : gives shape to the leaf
answer_explanation: Veins are responsible for the transport of water and nutrients, and they provide a framework to the leaf. Transpiration occurs through stomata.

subject: Science
question_text:
Read the following sentences about photosynthesis:
(i) Sunlight, carbon dioxide, chlorophyll and water are necessary.
(ii) Oxygen is absorbed.
(iii) Leaves carry out photosynthesis.
(iv) Proteins are made during photosynthesis.
Choose the correct pair of sentences that are true to photosynthesis
answer_explanation: (i) and (iii) are true. (ii) Oxygen is released, not absorbed. (iv) Carbohydrates (like starch) are produced, not proteins.

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following terms constitute the female part of the flower.
answer_explanation: The pistil (or carpel) is the female reproductive part of a flower, which consists of the stigma, style, and ovary.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following terms constitute the female part of the flower.

subject: Science
question_text:
Fill in the blanks:
(a) The small green leaves at the base of flowers are known as ________.
(b) The swollen basal part of the pistil is the ________ which bears the ________.
(c) Stamen has two parts called ________ and ________.
(d) The young unopened flower is termed as ________.
answer_key:
(a) sepals
(b) ovary; ovules
(c) anther; filament
(d) bud
provenance_excerpt:

subject: Science
question_text:
Solve the riddles given below:
(a) “I have a green tender stem and I am much shorter than you. Who am I?”
(b) I come out first from the seed when it is soaked in water. I provide anchorage to plants. Who am I? Write another function that I perform.
answer_key:
(a) Herb
(b) Root. Another function of the root is to absorb water and minerals from the soil.

subject: Science
question_text:
Match the parts of plant given in Column I with their function in Column II
Column I: (a) Flower (b) Leaf (c) Stem (d) Root
Column II: (i) Excretion (ii) Photosynthesis (iii) Reproduction (iv) Bears branches (v) Anchorage
answer_key:
(a) Flower - (iii) Reproduction
(b) Leaf - (ii) Photosynthesis
(c) Stem - (iv) Bears branches
(d) Root - (v) Anchorage
answer_explanation: Matching plant parts to their primary functions.

subject: Science
question_text:
Boojho wanted to test the presence of starch in leaves. He performed the following steps.
(1) He took a leaf and boiled it in water,
(2) He placed the leaf in a petri dish and poured some iodine over it.
He did not get the expected result. Which step did he miss? Explain.
answer_key: He missed the step of boiling the leaf in alcohol (using a water bath). Boiling the leaf in alcohol dissolves and removes the green pigment (chlorophyll). Without removing the green colour, the blue-black colour of the iodine starch test cannot be seen properly.
answer_explanation: Understanding the procedure for the starch test.

(1) He took a leaf and boiled

subject: Science
question_text: Will a leaf taken from a potted plant kept in a dark room for a few days turn blue black when tested for starch? Give reasons for your answer.
answer_key: No, it will not turn blue-black. Photosynthesis does not happen in the dark. The plant uses up the starch already stored in its leaves over a few days in the dark. Therefore, no starch remains to react with the iodine.

subject: Science
question_text: Can the stem of a plant be compared with a street with two–way traffic? Give reason.
answer_key: Yes. The stem acts like a two-way street because it transports water and minerals upwards from the roots to the leaves, and it also transports synthesized food from the leaves downwards to other parts of the plant.
answer_explanation: Functions of the stem in transport.
provenance_excerpt: Can the stem of a plant be compared with a street with two–way traffic? Give reason.

question_text:
Draw the veins of leaves given in Fig. 7.2 below and write the type of venation.
Grass Peepal China Rose
answer_key:
1. Grass: Parallel venation (veins run parallel to each other).
2. Peepal: Reticulate venation (veins form a net-like design).
3. China Rose: Reticulate venation.

subject: Science
question_text:
Observe the picture of an activity given as Fig. 7.4 carried out with leaves of plants and polythene bag. Now answer the following:
(a) Which process is demonstrated in the activity?
(b) When will this activity show better results – on a bright sunny day or a cloudy day?
(c) What will you observe in the polythene bag after a few hours of setting up the activity?
(d) Mention any one precaution you must take while performing this activity.
answer_key:
(a) Transpiration (loss of water vapour from leaves).
(b) It will show better results on a bright sunny day because the rate of transpiration is higher when there is more sunlight and heat.
(c) We will observe droplets of water on the inner surface of the polythene bag.
(d) Use a healthy, well-watered plant, and ensure the mouth of the polythene bag is tied tightly to prevent water vapour from escaping.

subject: Science
question_text:
Identify the wrong statements and correct them.
(a) Anther is a part of the pistil.
(b) The visible parts of a bud are the petals.
(c) Lateral roots are present in a tap root.
(d) Leaves perform the function of transpiration only.
answer_key:
Wrong statements: (a), (b), and (d).
Corrections:
(a) Anther is a part of the stamen (not pistil).
(b) The visible parts of a bud are the sepals (not petals).
(c) This statement is correct.
(d) Leaves perform photosynthesis and exchange of gases (respiration) in addition to transpiration.

(a) Anther is a part of the pistil.
(b) The visible parts of a bud are t

subject: Science
question_text:
Fill in the blanks with the terms that are listed below: anther, male, ovary, ovule, petals, pistil, stamen, filament.
Sepals, (a) _________, stamens and (b) _________ are the parts of a flower. Stamen is made up of (c) _________ and (d) _________ and it represents the (e) _________ part of the flower. The female part of the flower is called the (f) _________. The basal, swollen part of the pistil is called the (g) _________ which contains the (h) _________.
answer_key:
(a) petals
(b) pistil
(c) anther
(d) filament
(e) male
(f) pistil
(g) ovary
(h) ovule
